content.problemStatement:
Alice has an array AAA of length NNN. She is interested in finding the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1\leq i \lt j \leq N1‚â§i<j‚â§N and Ai‚äïAj<Ai&AjA_i \oplus A_j \lt A_i \& A_jAi‚Äã‚äïAj‚Äã<Ai‚Äã&Aj‚Äã.

‚äï\oplus‚äï represents the Bitwise XOR operator.
&\&& represents the Bitwise AND operator.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain an integer TTT ‚Äî number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, the length of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots , A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.


Output Format
For each test case, output the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1‚â§i<j‚â§N and Ai‚äïAj<Ai&AjA_i \oplus A_j \lt A_i \& A_jAi‚Äã‚äïAj‚Äã<Ai‚Äã&Aj‚Äã.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚ãÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The two pairs of indices satisfying the condition are (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) and (3,4)(3, 4)(3,4), because

A1‚äïA2=0A_1 \oplus A_2 = 0A1‚Äã‚äïA2‚Äã=0 and A1&A2=1A_1 \& A_2 = 1A1‚Äã&A2‚Äã=1
A3‚äïA4=1A_3 \oplus A_4 = 1A3‚Äã‚äïA4‚Äã=1 and A3&A4=2A_3 \& A_4 = 2A3‚Äã&A4‚Äã=2

Test case 222: The only pair of indices satisfying the condition is (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3).
